children.label: A
children.text: potential changes in lifecycle carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gas emissions; and

children.label: B
children.text: other environmental safety indicators of new technologies, practices, processes, or methods used in enhanced hydrocarbon recovery as part of the activities authorized under ;section 16293 of this title
to identify and assess novel uses for carbon, including the conversion of carbon and carbon oxides for commercial and industrial products and other products with potential market value;

to develop or obtain, in coordination with other applicable Federal agencies and standard-setting organizations, standards and certifications, as appropriate, to facilitate the commercialization of the products and technologies described in paragraph (2);
to identify and assess carbon capture technologies for industrial systems; and

to identify and assess alternative uses for raw coal and processed coal products in all phases that result in no significant emissions of carbon dioxide or other pollutants, including products derived from carbon engineering, carbon fiber, and coal conversion methods.

children.heading: Demonstration programs for the purpose of commercialization
children.label: b Demonstration programs for the purpose of commercialization

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , as part of the program established under subsection (a), the Secretary shall establish a 2-year demonstration program in each of the 2 major coal-producing regions of the United States for the purpose of partnering with private institutions in coal mining regions to accelerate the commercial deployment of coal-carbon products.December 27, 2020

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish a program to provide grants to eligible entities to use in accordance with subparagraph (D).November 15, 2021

children.label: i
children.text: a State;

children.label: ii
children.text: a unit of local government; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a public utility or agency.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: C Applications
children.text: Eligible entities desiring a grant under this paragraph shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: use or are derived from anthropogenic carbon oxides; and

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrate significant net reductions in lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions compared to incumbent technologies, processes, and products.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: 3 Cost sharing
children.text: Activities under this subsection shall be subject to the cost-sharing requirements of .section 16352 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: post-combustion and pre-combustion capture of carbon dioxide;

children.label: B
children.text: advanced compression technologies for new and existing fossil fuel-fired power plants;

children.label: C
children.text: technologies to convert carbon dioxide to valuable products and commodities; and

children.label: D
children.text: advanced carbon dioxide storage technologies that consider a range of storage regimes.

children.label: A
children.text: select the Center under this subsection on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis; and

children.label: B
children.text: consider applications from the National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, multiinstitutional collaborations, and other appropriate entities.

children.heading: Existing centers
children.label: 3 Existing centers
children.text: In selecting the Center under this subsection, the Secretary shall prioritize carbon utilization research centers in existence on .December 27, 2020

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 4 Duration
children.text: The Center established under this subsection shall receive support for a period of not more than 5 years, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: 5 Renewal
children.text: On the expiration of any period of support of the Center, the Secretary may renew support for the Center, on a merit-reviewed basis, for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 6 Termination
children.text: Consistent with the existing authorities of the Department, the Secretary may terminate the Center for cause during the performance period.
$41,000,000 for fiscal year 2022;
$65,250,000 for fiscal year 2023;
$66,562,500 for fiscal year 2024;
$67,940,625 for fiscal year 2025; and
$69,387,656 for fiscal year 2026.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: e Coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate the activities authorized in this section with the activities authorized in as part of one consolidated program at the Department. Nothing in shall be construed as limiting the authorities provided in this section.section 16298 of this titlesection 16298 of this title
